title: Cyrtochilum wetzelii
intro: Cyrtochilum wetzelii, also known as Wetzel's Cyrtochilum, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtochilum wetzelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cyrtochilum wetzelii is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, to support wound healing and immune system function.
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum wetzelii is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Cyrtochilum and the family Orchidaceae. Cyrtochilum wetzelii is a type of terrestrial orchid native to South America. This species is further classified as a flowering plant within the kingdom Plantae. Cyrtochilum wetzelii belongs to the order Asparagales and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Cyrtochilum wetzelii's classification at the level of superorder is Lilianae.
